name: Gate3587SuccessorBug
description:
Issue #3587 successor-Deacon scaling. Same single-pair topology as gate-3587-bug.kinner.json, plus a SECOND Deacon (Dec2) instance with its own Reaper. Demonstrates that a successor Deacon session CAN'T recover the bug state because its `bd gate check` only acts on Gate=Open gates -- the closed gate left behind by the crashed first Deacon is invisible.

The bug body (#3587) cites this filter as the load-bearing root cause: 'bd gate check only queries status=open gates. Once a gate is closed, it becomes invisible to new Deacon sessions.' The model encodes this as Deacon's Idle -> ClosingGate guard (gateState=Open). When Dec1 closes Gt and crashes, Gt=Closed, and Dec2's guard fails permanently.

Fan-in on Gt.CLOSE_IN (both Deacons could try to close) and Pol.WAKE_IN (both could send wake) is auto-synthesized by the compiler per backlog 074.

Liveness violates as before -- both Deacons can crash via their Reapers, and the closed gate prevents any successor recovery.

properties.description: Liveness: under fairness, the parked polecat eventually reaches Working. Fails because the bug state (Polecat=Parked, Gt=Closed, Dec1=Crashed) is reachable, and Dec2 can't help (its Idle->ClosingGate guard requires gateState=Open, but the gate is already Closed).
